## 2. Morphological Overview

### 2.1 Word Classes [✓]

| POS | Lemmas | Tokens |
|-----|--------|--------|
| N | 2,146 | 218,581 |
| V | 773 | 148,757 |
| PROP | 4,060 | 58,452 |
| PRON | 304 | 35,822 |
| CONJ | 9 | 35,297 |
| DET | 13 | 29,442 |
| ADJ | 13 | 16,219 |
| NUM | 11 | 11,216 |
| ADV | 10 | 6,908 |


### 2.2 Morphological Processes [✓]

Major morphological processes:

- **Prefixation:** pronominal agreement (ka-, na-, a-)
- **Suffixation:** TAM, case, nominalization
- **Compounding:** N+N, V+N, N+V
- **Reduplication:** intensity, plurality


### 2.3 Compounding [✓]

The corpus contains **0** compound entries.



### 2.4 Reduplication [✓]

Reduplication marks intensity or plurality:

- **zel~zel** "very far" (from zel "far")
- **nuam~nuam** "comfortable" (from nuam "pleasant")

## 3. Noun Morphology

### 3.1 Noun Classification [✓]

The corpus contains **2,146** noun lemmas.



### 3.2 Plural Marking [✓]

This section documents 6 items in the **plural_marker** category.


| Form | Gloss | Function | Position | Frequency |
|------|-------|----------|----------|-----------|
| te | PL | PL | suffix | 28,507 |
| te | TE | TE | suffix | 474 |
| te | PL.POSS | PL.POSS | suffix | 466 |
| te | TE.POSS | TE.POSS | suffix | 299 |
| uh | PL | PL | suffix | 115 |
| te | LOC | LOC | suffix | 3 |

**Example: te 'PL'**

- Pasian in van kuumpi bawl a, van kuumpi tunga om tuite le van kuumpi nuai-a om tuite a khen hi. Tua mah bangin a piang pah hi.
  - *tui-te* → 'water-PL'
  - 'And God made the firmament, and divided the waters which were under the firmament from the waters which were above the firmament: and it was so.'
  - (Genesis 1:7)

- Pasian in, “Leitung in kung tuamtuamte piangsak hen la, tang a nei kungnote, a gah sungah tang a om singgah kungte, amau' nam ciatin leitungah piangsak hen,” ci hi. Tua mah bangin a piang pah hi.
  - *tuam-tuam-te* → 'different-REDUP-PL'
  - 'And God said, Let the earth bring forth grass, the herb yielding seed, and the fruit tree yielding fruit after his kind, whose seed is in itself, upon the earth: and it was so.'
  - (Genesis 1:11)


### 3.3 Case Marking [✓]

This section documents 12 items in the **case_marker** category.


| Form | Gloss | Function | Position | Frequency |
|------|-------|----------|----------|-----------|
| in | ERG | ERG | suffix | 77,717 |
| ah | LOC | LOC | suffix | 24,889 |
| tawh | COM | COM | suffix | 7,808 |
| pan | ABL | ABL | suffix | 4,944 |
| in | CVB | CVB | suffix | 4,331 |
| in | QUOT | QUOT | suffix | 1,938 |
| in | INST | INST | suffix | 701 |
| in | IN | IN | suffix | 102 |
| in | leaf | leaf | suffix | 2 |
| in | fly | fly | suffix | 2 |
| in | with | with | suffix | 2 |
| in | take | take | suffix | 1 |

**Example: in 'ERG'**

- Pasian in, “Khuavak om hen,” ci hi; tua ciangin khuavak om pah hi.
  - *ciang-in* → 'then-ERG'
  - 'And God said, Let there be light: and there was light.'
  - (Genesis 1:3)

- Pasian in tua khuavak hoih hi, ci-in a mu hi. Pasian in khuamial panin khuavak khenkhia hi.
  - *pan-in* → 'ABL-ERG'
  - 'And God saw the light, that it was good: and God divided the light from the darkness.'
  - (Genesis 1:4)


### 3.4 Possession [⚠]

> **Provisional:** Needs review.


Possession is marked by pronominal prefixes on possessed nouns:

- **ka-** (1SG): ka-inn "my house"
- **na-** (2SG): na-inn "your house"
- **a-** (3SG): a-inn "his/her house"


### 3.5 Nominalization [✓]

This section documents 1 items in the **nominalizer** category.


| Form | Gloss | Function | Position | Frequency |
|------|-------|----------|----------|-----------|
| na | NMLZ | NMLZ | suffix | 22,902 |

**Example: na 'NMLZ'**

- A deihhuai, nek dinga a hoih singkung nam khempeuh, huan laizangah nuntakna singkung, le a pha le a sia theihna singkung, lei sung panin Topa Pasian in posak hi.
  - *nuntak-na* → 'live-NMLZ'
  - 'And out of the ground made the LORD God to grow every tree that is pleasant to the sight, and good for food; the tree of life also in the midst of the garden, and the tree of knowledge of good and evil.'
  - (Genesis 2:9)

- Leitung in limlemel nei loin a awngthawlpi ahi hi. Tuipi tung tengah khua mial bikbek a, tua tui tungah Pasian' Kha in na a sem hi.
  - *na* → '2SG'
  - 'And the earth was without form, and void; and darkness was upon the face of the deep. And the Spirit of God moved upon the face of the waters.'
  - (Genesis 1:2)

### 4.2 Pronominal Prefixes [✓]

This section documents 5 items in the **pronominal_prefix** category.


| Form | Gloss | Function | Position | Frequency |
|------|-------|----------|----------|-----------|
| a | 3SG | 3SG | prefix | 77,812 |
| na | 2SG | 2SG | prefix | 18,661 |

**Example: a '3SG'**

- Pasian in tua khuavak hoih hi, ci-in a mu hi. Pasian in khuamial panin khuavak khenkhia hi.
  - *a* → '3SG'
  - 'And God saw the light, that it was good: and God divided the light from the darkness.'
  - (Genesis 1:4)

- Pasian in khuavak pen “Sun” ci a, khuamial pen “Zan” ci hi. Nitak hong bei-in, zingsang hong tung a, ni khat ni ahi hi.
  - *a* → '3SG'
  - 'And God called the light Day, and the darkness he called Night. And the evening and the morning were the first day.'
  - (Genesis 1:5)


### 4.3 Demonstratives [⚠]

> **Provisional:** Needs review.


Demonstrative pronouns distinguish proximal and distal:

- **hi** — proximal "this"
- **tua** — distal "that"


### 4.4 Interrogatives [⚠]

> **Provisional:** Needs review.


Question words include:

- **bang** — "what"
- **kua** — "who"
- **koizah** — "where"
- **banghang** — "why"

## 5. Verb Morphology

### 5.1 Verb Classification [✓]

The corpus contains **773** verb lemmas.


Key verb categories:
- **Motion verbs:** pai (go), hong (come), lut (enter)
- **Stance verbs:** om (exist), ding (stand)
- **Perception verbs:** mu (see), za (know), thei (know)
- **Speech verbs:** ci (say), gen (speak)


### 5.2 Stem Alternations [✓]

Tedim Chin verbs show systematic stem alternations:


| Stem I | Stem II | Gloss |
|--------|---------|-------|
| mu | muh | see |
| za | zak | know/hear |
| thei | theih | know/can |
| ne | neh | eat |
| gel | geel | write |


### 5.3 TAM Suffixes [✓]

This section documents 9 items in the **tam_suffix** category.


| Form | Gloss | Function | Position | Frequency |
|------|-------|----------|----------|-----------|
| sak | CAUS | CAUS | suffix | 5,807 |
| pih | APPL | APPL | suffix | 1,898 |
| ta | PFV | PFV | suffix | 979 |
| zo | COMPL | COMPL | suffix | 702 |
| khin | COMPL | COMPL | suffix | 271 |
| lai | still | still | suffix | 258 |
| kik | again | again | suffix | 131 |
| gige | HAB | HAB | suffix | 20 |
| pah | immediately | immediately | suffix | 4 |

**Example: sak 'CAUS'**

- Huan sung a kawtsak dingin Eden nawkin gun khat hong luang a, tua mun panin gun hong kikhenin li hong pha hi.
  - *kawt-sak* → 'garden-CAUS'
  - 'And a river went out of Eden to water the garden; and from thence it was parted, and became into four heads.'
  - (Genesis 2:10)

- Topa Pasian in leitungah guah zusak nai lo a, leitang a kho ding mi om lo ahih manin gam sung kungnote lei sungah om nai lo a, lo sung ankungte pokhia lo hi.
  - *zu-sak* → 'wine-CAUS'
  - 'and every plant of the field before it was in the earth, and every herb of the field before it grew: for the LORD God had not caused it to rain upon the earth, and there was not a man to till the ground.'
  - (Genesis 2:5)


### 5.4 Irrealis Marking [✓]

This section documents 3 items in the **irrealis_marker** category.


| Form | Gloss | Function | Position | Frequency |
|------|-------|----------|----------|-----------|
| ding | IRR | IRR | suffix | 24,982 |
| ding | tree | tree | suffix | 3 |
| ding | FUT | FUT | suffix | 1 |

**Example: ding 'IRR'**

- Hih kulh cianna-ah kei mahmah in zong ka sem a, kei a dingin leitang bangmah ka la kei hi. Keima mite khempeuh nasem ding bekin tua lai-ah kisutuah uh hi.
  - *ding-in* → 'IRR-ERG'
  - 'Yea, also I continued in the work of this wall, neither bought we any land: and all my servants were gathered thither unto the work.'
  - (Nehemiah 5:16)

- “Mualte a dingin kapin tau unla, gamlak lonona munte a dingin thumin tau un. Bang hang hiam cih leh tuate keu gawp a, kuamah pai nawn lo hi. Bawng ham zong kiza nawn lo a, vana leeng vasate le gamsate a taimangta uh hi.
  - *ding-in* → 'IRR-ERG'
  - 'For the mountains will I take up a weeping and wailing, and for the habitations of the wilderness a lamentation, because they are burned up, so that none can pass through them ; neither can men hear the voice of the cattle; both the fowl of the heavens and the beast are fled; they are gone.'
  - (Jeremiah 9:10)


### 5.5 Sentence-Final Particles [✓]

This section documents 4 items in the **sentence_final** category.


| Form | Gloss | Function | Position | Frequency |
|------|-------|----------|----------|-----------|
| hi | DECL | DECL | suffix | 47,720 |
| hiam | Q | Q | suffix | 5,230 |
| ang | FUT | FUT | suffix | 32 |
| eh | EXCL | EXCL | suffix | 6 |

**Example: hi 'DECL'**

- “Tho inla, Samaria-ah a om Israel kumpi Ahab a dawntuah dingin paisuk in. Naboth' leenggui lo a la dingin tua lai-ah amah om hi.
  - *hi'* → 'DECL.POSS'
  - 'Arise, go down to meet Ahab king of Israel, which is in Samaria: behold, he is in the vineyard of Naboth, whither he is gone down to possess it.'
  - (1 Kings 21:18)

- “Ka galte'n gim a thuak teh ka kipak ngei kei a, haksatna a tuah uh teh ka lungdam ngei kei hi.
  - *hi'* → 'DECL.POSS'
  - 'If I rejoiced at the destruction of him that hated me, Or lifted up myself when evil found him:'
  - (Job 31:29)

This section documents 7 items in the **directional_verb** category.


| Form | Gloss | Function | Position | Frequency |

| tung | arrive | arrive | suffix | 136 |
| hong | come | come | suffix | 25 |
| hong | open | open | suffix | 4 |
| hong | keep | keep | suffix | 1 |

**Example: pai 'go'**

- Ahi zongin a sanggampa le a nu in, “A tawm penin ni sawm bang, ko tawh numei hong omsak lai in; tua khit ciangin amah hong pai thei ding hi,” a ci uh hi.
  - *Pai* → 'go'
  - 'And her brother and her mother said, Let the damsel abide with us a few days, at the least ten; after that she shall go.'
  - (Genesis 24:55)

- Abraham' tungah a thugen ding a man khit ciangin ama pai nadingah Topa pai a, Abraham zong ama inn-ah a ciahkik hi.
  - *Pai* → 'go'
  - 'And the LORD went his way, as soon as he had left communing with Abraham: and Abraham returned unto his place.'
  - (Genesis 18:33)

> **Provisional:** Needs review.


This section documents 3 items in the **auxiliary** category.


| Form | Gloss | Function | Position | Frequency |

| thei | ABIL | ABIL | suffix | 219 |
| theih | ABIL.II | ABIL.II | suffix | 11 |

**Example: nuam 'want'**

- (thunuama biakna bawngtal panin hihte a kilak mah bangin) la ding a, siampipa in meihal biakna tau tungah tuate hal ding hi.
  - *thu-nuam-a* → 'word-want-LOC'
  - 'as it was taken off from the bullock of the sacrifice of peace offerings: and the priest shall burn them upon the altar of the burnt offering.'
  - (Leviticus 4:10)

- “Mi khat' biakna pen thunuama biakna ahih a, amah in a nu hitaleh a pa hitaleh bawnghon lak panin a piak leh, amah in Topa' mai-ah a sitbaang lo pia ding hi.
  - *thu-nuam-a* → 'word-want-LOC'
  - 'And if his oblation be a sacrifice of peace offering, if he offer it of the herd; whether it be a male or female, he shall offer it without blemish before the LORD .'
  - (Leviticus 3:1)

## 6. Function Words

### 6.1 Overview [✓]

The grammar includes **365** function words.



### 6.2 Copula [✓]

This section documents 2 items in the **copula** category.


| Form | Gloss | Function | Position | Frequency |
|------|-------|----------|----------|-----------|
| ahi | be.3SG | be.3SG | suffix | 8,634 |
| ahih | be.3SG.REL | be.3SG.REL | suffix | 5,804 |

**Example: ahi 'be.3SG'**

- Ahih hangin ka kikimpih, ka kikholhpih, ka lawm theih mahmah, nangmah na hi hi.
  - *Ahih* → 'be.3SG.REL'
  - 'But it was thou, a man mine equal, My guide, and mine acquaintance.'
  - (Psalms 55:13)

- Tua ahih ciangin mi khempeuh in amau a' ding ciat selkeu tomin Abimelek zui-in munmuanhuai sim nadingin koih uh a, amaute in mei tawh munmuanhuai halin Shekhem Tausang mite, numei le pasal mi tulkhat bang a si hi.
  - *Ahih* → 'be.3SG.REL'
  - 'And all the people likewise cut down every man his bough, and followed Abimelech, and put them to the hold, and set the hold on fire upon them; so that all the men of the tower of Shechem died also, about a thousand men and women.'
  - (Judges 9:49)

### 6.4 Discourse Particles [⚠]

> **Provisional:** Needs review.


This section documents 9 items in the **particle** category.


| Form | Gloss | Function | Position | Frequency |
|------|-------|----------|----------|-----------|
| le | and | and | suffix | 11,339 |
| pen | TOP | TOP | suffix | 4,815 |
| zong | also | also | suffix | 3,512 |
| leh | and | and | suffix | 3,402 |
| mah | EMPH | EMPH | suffix | 1,992 |
| bek | only | only | suffix | 805 |
| peuh | every | every | suffix | 337 |
| leh | if | if | suffix | 321 |
| peuh | any | any | suffix | 7 |

**Example: le 'and'**

- Pasian in mi khat neihlelam guanin, a hauhna nuamtakin a neksak leh, amah lungdamin a sepna thaman ne-in a om ding hi. Tua nopsakna pen Pasian' kiang pan ahi hi.
  - *neih-le-lam* → 'have-also-direction'
  - 'Every man also to whom God hath given riches and wealth, and hath given him power to eat thereof, and to take his portion, and to rejoice in his labour; this is the gift of God.'
  - (Ecclesiastes 5:19)

- Pasian in mi khatpeuh tungah neihlelam, hauhna, le pahtawina pia-in, a deih khempeuh neisak napi-in, tua nate nuamtakin zangsak loin, midang khat in nopsaklawh laizang hi. Hih pen a mawknapi, a thuman het lo khat ahi hi.
  - *neih-le-lam* → 'have-also-direction'
  - 'a man to whom God hath given riches, wealth, and honour, so that he wanteth nothing for his soul of all that he desireth, yet God giveth him not power to eat thereof, but a stranger eateth it: this is vanity, and it is an evil disease.'
  - (Ecclesiastes 6:2)

## 7. Clause Structure

### 7.1 Basic Clause Types [⚠]

> **Provisional:** Needs review.


Tedim Chin has declarative, interrogative, and imperative clauses.

- **Declarative:** Verb-final with sentence-final particle
- **Interrogative:** Question word or rising intonation
- **Imperative:** Bare stem or with imperative particle


### 7.2 Argument Structure [⚠]

> **Provisional:** Needs review.


Tedim Chin shows ergative-absolutive alignment:

- Transitive agent marked with **-in** (ERG)
- Intransitive subject and transitive patient unmarked


### 7.3 Word Order [❌]

> **GAP:** This section needs to be written.

> **GAP:** This section needs to be written.

## 8. Complex Sentences

### 8.1 Coordination [⚠]

> **Provisional:** Needs review.


Coordination uses **le** "and" and **ahibale** "but":

- NP le NP "NP and NP"
- Clause, ahibale clause "Clause, but clause"
